questions.question: The Wechsler Adult Intelligence Scale (WAIS) yields which type of intelligence score?

choices.B: Deviation IQ based on a mean of 100 and SD of 15
choices.C: Percentile rank relative to age peers
choices.D: T-score with mean 50 and SD of 10

questions.explanation: The WAIS uses deviation IQ (developed by David Wechsler), where IQ scores are computed relative to one's age group with a mean of 100 and standard deviation of 15. A score of 115 means one SD above average.

questions.question: The Rorschach Inkblot Test is classified as a:
questions.explanation: Projective tests (like Rorschach, TAT) present ambiguous stimuli that subjects interpret based on their own unconscious projections, revealing personality structures, conflicts, and dynamics. They contrast with objective tests (MMPI) that use structured items and standardized scoring.

questions.question: Item discrimination index refers to:

choices.A: The difficulty level of a test item
choices.B: How well a test item differentiates high from low performers
choices.C: The correlation of a test with an external criterion
choices.D: The number of distractors in a multiple-choice item

questions.explanation: Item discrimination index (D) measures how well an item differentiates between high-scoring and low-scoring test takers. Computed as the difference in proportion correct between the upper and lower performance groups. Ideal D is 0.30 or higher.

questions.question: Test-retest reliability is best assessed by:

choices.A: Correlating two halves of the same test
choices.B: Administering the same test to the same group at two different times
choices.C: Comparing scores across different forms of a test
choices.D: Examining the internal consistency of test items

questions.explanation: Test-retest reliability assesses temporal stability by correlating scores from the same test administered to the same group at two different time points. High reliability indicates consistent measurement over time.

questions.question: The MMPI-2 clinical scale that measures psychopathic deviate characteristics — including conflict with authority, social alienation, and impulsive behavior — is Scale:
questions.explanation: MMPI-2 Scale 4 (Pd - Psychopathic Deviate) measures characteristics associated with antisocial behavior, including poor impulse control, conflict with authority, family alienation, social imperturbability, and amorality.

questions.question: The Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE) is primarily used to screen for:
questions.explanation: The MMSE is a brief 30-point questionnaire widely used to screen for cognitive impairment and monitor dementia progression. It assesses orientation, registration, attention, recall, and language.

questions.question: The principle of informed consent in psychological assessment requires that clients be told about:

choices.B: The purpose, nature, and use of assessment results
choices.C: The cost of the assessment only

questions.explanation: Ethical guidelines require psychologists to obtain informed consent by explaining the nature and purpose of the assessment, how results will be used, who will have access to the results, and any limitations of the assessment.

questions.question: A normal distribution has a mean of 100 and standard deviation of 15. What percentage of scores fall between 85 and 115?
questions.explanation: In a normal distribution, approximately 68% of scores fall within one standard deviation of the mean (±1 SD). Mean ± 1 SD = 100 ± 15 = 85 to 115. This is the 68-95-99.7 rule.

questions.question: The Thematic Apperception Test (TAT) requires subjects to:

choices.A: Sort cards based on color, shape, or number
choices.B: Tell a story about ambiguous pictures showing people in various situations
choices.D: Describe what they see in inkblots

questions.explanation: The TAT (Murray & Morgan) presents ambiguous pictures of people in various social situations; subjects create a story with a beginning, middle, and end. The stories are analyzed for themes related to needs, motives, and interpersonal relationships.

questions.question: Which of the following represents an IQ score that falls in the 'intellectually disabled' range?
questions.explanation: Intellectual Disability (formerly Mental Retardation) is diagnosed when IQ is below approximately 70 (i.e., more than 2 SD below mean of 100), accompanied by significant deficits in adaptive functioning, with onset during the developmental period.
